question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling task execution to observe that EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() skips execution when running.compareAndExchange(false, true) returns true, and use repeated rapid API calls to keep the scheduler busy, preventing the pending_reward from being updated for an entire staking period, causing stakers to lose one period's worth of rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with a future timestamp (e.g., timestamp=lte:9999999999.999999999) to /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards to retrieve all staking reward transfers including any that may have been pre-inserted or incorrectly timestamped, exposing reward records that should not yet be visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the proxy_staking CTE in updateEntityStake() by creating an account that stakes to a high-value account (staked_account_id), causing the staked_to_me balance to be inflated in the entity_state_start view, which then increases the stake_total_start for the victim account and artificially inflates the victim's pending_reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the forfeited_period CTE in updateEntityStake() by observing that rewards older than 365 days are deducted, and use the /api/v1/accounts/{id} endpoint to monitor when an account's stake_period_start approaches the 365-day boundary, timing on-chain transactions to claim rewards just before they are forfeited?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the first_period_stake_change CTE in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration.doMigrate() by observing that crypto transfers between the last staking reward transfer and the first period end timestamp affect the stake_total_start used for reward calculation, and use the /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards endpoint to identify accounts with large pending rewards that are vulnerable to stake dilution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account ID in the format shard.realm.num (e.g., 0.0.1001) versus just num (e.g., 1001) to /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards and exploit any inconsistency in how EntityService.getEncodedId() resolves these formats to access reward records for a different account than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pending_reward CASE expression condition (e.stake_period_start >= es.end_stake_period then 0) by querying /api/v1/accounts/{id} with a timestamp parameter that selects a historical entity row where stake_period_start was less than end_stake_period, causing the API to return a non-zero pending_reward for a period when the account had already been paid, creating a false impression of unclaimed rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication component scan to discover and invoke an unintended REST endpoint that directly exposes the entity_stake table contents including pending_reward, staked_node_id_start, and stake_total_start for all accounts, enabling bulk enumeration of unpaid staking rewards across the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: lookup()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a zero-padded EVM address (e.g., 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000320 for account 800) to EntityIdEvmAddressParameter to resolve to the staking reward account 0.0.800, and then use the resolved entity ID to query internal staking state that reveals the total reserved_staking_rewards and unreserved_staking_reward_balance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generation in listStakingRewardsByAccountId() by manipulating the timestamp value in the next link to skip backward in time, causing the API to return reward records that were already returned in a previous page, leading to double-counting of rewards in client-side aggregations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableAsync thread pool configuration to submit a large number of concurrent requests to /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards, causing the async processing queue to overflow and drop reward calculation tasks, resulting in stale pending_reward values being served indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entity_stake table's lack of row-level security by querying /api/v1/accounts/{id} for any account ID and reading the pending_reward field, enabling systematic enumeration of all accounts with non-zero pending rewards to identify high-value targets for social engineering or on-chain attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isValidAccount() in listStakingRewardsByAccountId() only checks entity existence (not deletion status or account type), allowing the attacker to query staking reward transfers for system accounts or deleted accounts that should not be accessible via the public API?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @EnableScheduling annotation to observe the periodic execution of EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() by monitoring response time variations on the /api/v1/accounts/{id} endpoint, and use this timing information to predict when pending_reward values will next be updated, enabling precise timing of on-chain staking changes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lockFromConcurrentUpdates() NOWAIT lock in EntityStakeRepository by sending requests that trigger concurrent entity_stake reads during the lock window, causing the lock acquisition to fail and the pending_reward update to be skipped for that staking period, resulting in stakers not receiving their earned rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the staked_to_me proxy_staking calculation by creating many small accounts that all stake to a single victim account (staked_account_id), artificially inflating the victim's stake_total_start in entity_state_start, causing the victim's pending_reward to be calculated on a larger stake than they actually control, and then withdrawing the proxy stakes to cause a sudden drop in the victim's reward?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FIRST_NONZERO_REWARD_RATE_TIMESTAMP hardcoded values in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration to infer that accounts created before these timestamps on MAINNET (1666310400447390002) or TESTNET (1659139200596847383) may have incorrectly calculated pending rewards, and use the /api/v1/accounts/{id} endpoint to identify such accounts with inflated pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with nanosecond precision (e.g., timestamp=gt:1234567890.000000001) to /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards to exploit off-by-one errors in the consensus_timestamp comparison, causing reward records at the exact boundary timestamp to be included or excluded incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SpringBootApplication auto-configuration of Spring Security (if absent or misconfigured) to access the /actuator/scheduledtasks endpoint, revealing the cron schedule of EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() and enabling precise timing attacks against the staking reward update window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pending_reward CASE expression condition (coalesce(e.staked_node_id, -1) = -1 then 0) by querying /api/v1/accounts/{id} for an account that has staked_node_id set to NULL in the entity table but has a non-zero pending_reward in entity_stake, causing the API to return 0 while the actual pending reward is non-zero, hiding the true reward state from the account owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: lookup()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted alias that is a valid ED25519 public key alias for an account that has a large pending_reward, and use EntityServiceImpl.lookup() via the /api/v1/accounts/{alias}/rewards endpoint to enumerate all historical reward payouts for that account, revealing the account's complete staking reward history?
